evidence.value: score_stock()=5.02/10 for 'AAPL' as of last point-in-time trading day on/before 2024-01-15

q3.explanation: Evolved over 41 real factor weights (technical + fundamental) in a 74-field DNA genome, per upstream's own code — NOT the 484 figure in upstream's marketing README, which does not match the actual executed engine (see adapter header, 'the marketed 484 factors figure...'). Backtest/walk-forward performance metrics for this genome (annual_return/sharpe/win_rate) are deliberately not surfaced here — see code comment on the Q5 exclusion rule.

universe_policy.selector_description: Real upstream selection rule (finclaw.evolution.auto_evolve.AutoEvolver.evaluate()'s inline backtest): each rebalance day, every stock in the loaded data pool is scored via score_stock(), filtered to score >= min_score (adaptively raised in bear-breadth regimes), cross-sectionally z-score-neutralized once >=5 candidates are scored that day, then the top dna.max_positions=7 candidates by score are bought — further reduced under market_regime_sensitivity=0.8077596914825184 weak-breadth conditions, or skipped entirely to 100% cash when breadth < min_breadth_to_trade=0.0. This is real, upstream-enforced multi-asset rotation logic, not fabricated. HOWEVER: this adapter's own translation only ever feeds AutoEvolver a small internal companion pool (requested ticker + up to 8 large-cap DEFAULT_US_SYMBOLS — see _run_evolution_for_window) so the GA has cross-sectional statistics to score against, and this adapter's own q3_signal()/q4_policy() report only the single requested asset 'AAPL' back to the harness (context.scope=ASSET) — this call is NOT itself a multi-asset portfolio-selection result, only a reflection of the real mechanism the underlying genome would run if given a full universe.

observation_policy.lookback_window: [2023-06-01, 2024-01-15] (real yfinance OHLCV window used to re-evolve this genome; see _run_evolution_for_window)
observation_policy.observation_description: score_stock() reads a 41-dimension real w_* factor set (technical indicators from upstream's own compute_rsi/compute_macd/compute_bollinger_bands/compute_kdj/compute_obv_trend/... functions, plus fundamental factors that are evolved but score as neutral 0.5 here since this adapter passes an empty fundamentals dict — see _score_ticker()). `features` above lists only the top-3-|weight| factors in this specific evolved genome, not the full input space.

decision_policy.decision_rule: Real upstream finclaw.evolution.scoring.score_stock(): a normalized weighted composite over 41 real technical+fundamental component scores (each raw component in [0,1]; 4 stub indicators — w_adx/w_vwap/w_ichimoku/w_elder_ray — are always forced to 0 effective weight regardless of their DNA value, per score_stock()'s own _effective_stubs set) -> score = 10 * sum(w_i * component_i) / sum(w_i), clamped to [0, 10]. A candidate is only selected for entry once this score >= dna.min_score (4) (upstream evaluate()'s adaptive_min_score gate, before any bear-regime raise via bear_min_score_add=1.9372852084892576). Real top-|weight| evolved factors for this genome: w_bollinger=0.1329, w_cci=0.1301, w_debt_ratio=0.1127.
decision_policy.output_semantics: per-asset composite entry score in [0, 10] (upstream score_stock()) gated by min_score, not a portfolio target weight; realized entries are T+1-open-price buys of the top-scoring candidates (upstream evaluate()/_run_backtest).
decision_policy.rebalance_frequency: every 2d (dna.hold_days) — upstream re-scores the full candidate pool and re-picks positions each hold period.

additional_constraints: stop_loss_per_day=1.7025117161144105 (adaptive: effective stop = min(stop_loss_per_day * hold_days, stop_loss_pct=21.221407173860467), real upstream evaluate())
take_profit_pct=58.72743016986357 scaled by profit_target_scaling=0.3723079696589228 (signal-strength-scaled target, real upstream evaluate())
trailing_stop_enabled=0.15151580695560585 (<=0.5 => disabled per upstream's own convention; no trailing stop applied)
time_decay_exit=0.20646060962395607 (progressively tightens the stop loss after half the hold period, real upstream evaluate())
market_regime_sensitivity=0.8077596914825184 (reduces effective max concurrent positions in weak-breadth regimes, real upstream evaluate())
bear_stop_loss_mult=0.9635043476216087 (tightens stop_loss_pct in bear-breadth regimes, real upstream evaluate(); 1.0 = no change is upstream's own no-op convention)
bear_take_profit_mult=0.8694161038229414 (lowers the take_profit_pct target in bear-breadth regimes, real upstream evaluate(); 1.0 = no change is upstream's own no-op convention)
bear_hold_days_mult=0.4555272369093765 (shortens hold_days in bear-breadth regimes, real upstream evaluate(); 1.0 = no change is upstream's own no-op convention)

artifact.description: Real evolved finclaw StrategyDNA genome (74 fields: min_score entry threshold, position sizing, stop_loss_pct/take_profit_pct/trailing-stop rules, hold_days, max_positions, kelly_fraction, and 41 w_* technical+fundamental factor weights) for 'AAPL' (fallback for requested 'AAPL'), from upstream AutoEvolver.evolve() (population=24, generations=30, seed=42) over generation_window [2023-06-01, 2024-01-15]. Selected internally by upstream's own walk-forward-based GA fitness ranking; the genome's backtest performance metrics (annual_return/sharpe/win_rate) are not reported here — see the 'No Q5' exclusion rule this migration follows (those belong to an evaluation layer, not to Q4's policy artifact).

q4.explanation: Q3 exposes this same GA's live per-day score_stock() reading; Q4 exposes the underlying evolved StrategyDNA genome itself as a frozen, serializable policy artifact — evolved once over the harness-supplied generation_window and not updated online by this adapter (upstream's own re-evolution is a separate, explicit CLI invocation, not something this adapter triggers automatically). In addition to the opaque artifact above, the honestly-mappable real DNA fields are also surfaced structurally below (universe_policy, observation_policy, decision_policy, constraints) — see each sub-field's own text for the exact real upstream source read for each mapping.
